name: rivetkit
description: RivetKit backend and Rivet Actor runtime guidance. Use for building, modifying, debugging, or testing Rivet Actors, registries, serverless/runner modes, deployment, or actor-based workflows.

references.name: actors/access-control
references.title: Access Control
references.description: Authorize actions, queue publishes, and event subscriptions with explicit hooks.

references.name: actors/actions
references.title: Actions
references.description: Actions are how your backend, frontend, or other actors can communicate with actors.

references.name: general/actor-configuration
references.title: Actor Configuration
references.description: This page documents the configuration options available when defining a RivetKit actor. The actor configuration is passed to the `actor()` function.

references.name: actors/keys
references.title: Actor Keys
references.description: Actor keys uniquely identify actor instances within each actor type. Keys are used for addressing which specific actor to communicate with.

references.name: actors/schedule
references.title: Actor Scheduling
references.description: Schedule actor actions in the future with persistent timers that survive restarts and upgrades.

references.title: Actor Statuses
references.description: Understand the lifecycle statuses of Rivet Actors, what they mean, how they appear in the API, and how to troubleshoot common issues.

references.name: actors/ai-and-user-generated-actors
references.title: AI and User-Generated Rivet Actors
references.description: This guide shows you how to programmatically create sandboxed Rivet environments and deploy custom actor code to them.

references.name: general/architecture
references.title: Architecture
references.description: - rivetkit is the typescript library used for both local development & to connect your application to rivet - a rivetkit instance is called a "runner." you can run multiple runners to scale rivetkit horiziotnally. read omre about runners below.

references.title: Ephemeral Variables
references.description: In addition to persisted state, Rivet provides a way to store ephemeral data that is not saved to permanent storage using `vars`. This is useful for temporary data that only needs to exist while the actor is running or data that cannot be serialized.

references.name: actors/postgres

references.description: While actors can serve as a complete database solution, they can also complement your existing databases. For example, you might use actors to handle frequently-changing data that needs real-time access, while keeping less frequently accessed data in your traditional database.
